impact: `*, *::before, *::after { border-radius: 0 !important }` (base.html:53-56) is too aggressive — it strips radii from Chart.js tooltip bubbles, SortableJS drag-ghosts, any future shadcn/Material-class imports, and notification-bell SVG paths. Symptom is visible at base.html:470 where `.b-dot` has to override the reset with its OWN `border-radius: 50% !important` just to draw circles. Other future round elements will need the same workaround. Recommended: scope the reset to project elements only (`.b-card, .b-btn, .b-row, [class^="b-"] { border-radius: 0; }`) and rely on the `b-*` design-system tokens. Or introduce a `--radius: 0` CSS variable, set the default and let widgets opt in.
title: Universal `border-radius: 0 !important` overrides third-party widgets
